23.reason: // The increment of i happens after checking for newline, so the last valid term is stored at index i, but i is not incremented for it. This causes num1 = i to miss the last term.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  // The increment of i happens after checking for newline, so the last valid term is stored at index i, but i is not incremented for it. This causes num1 = i to miss the last term.

35.reason: // Same issue as above: j is not incremented after reading the last term, so num2 = j misses the last term.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  // Same issue as above: j is not incremented after reading the last term, so num2 = j misses the last term.

47.reason: // Because i was not incremented after the last successful read, num1 is one less than the actual number of terms.

48.reason: // Same issue: num2 is one less than the actual number of terms in the second polynomial.

65.reason: // Setting coefficient to zero instead of removing the term leads to potential output of zero-coefficient terms if not handled later, but more critically, this naive combination doesn't merge all like terms correctly (e.g., three terms with same exponent).

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  // Setting coefficient to zero instead of removing the term leads to potential output of zero-coefficient terms if not handled later, but more critically, this naive combination doesn't merge all like terms correctly (e.g., three terms with same exponent).

75.reason: // Output order is not guaranteed to be descending by exponent; problem requires terms printed in descending order of exponents.
